title: [lichen_l2.c:506] peer_try_all_pubkeys modifies ctx then restores on error - complex state management
description:
Contract: peer_try_all_pubkeys should return ctx in known state on all paths.
Violation: The function saves ctx->peer_pubkey and ctx->peer_eui64 (lines 514-515), modifies them during iteration (lines 544-545), and restores on error (lines 570-573, 592-594). On success, it re-sets them to the matched peer's values (lines 583-584). This modify-then-restore pattern is correct but complex.
Impact: The comment (lines 481-489) correctly explains why this is safe (ctx is stack-local, caller holds rx_mutex). However, if ctx is ever passed from external state or if locking discipline changes, the pattern becomes unsafe.
Mitigation: The comments are adequate. Consider refactoring to return matched peer info separately rather than modifying ctx, for cleaner contract.
close_reason: Verified correctness: (1) ctx is stack-local in lichen_l2_input (line 1774), (2) caller holds rx_mutex (line 1711), (3) all error paths restore saved state (lines 575-577, 597-599), (4) success path correctly updates to matched peer (lines 588-590). Comments at lines 485-500 accurately document the safety invariants. The modify-then-restore pattern is correct and well-documented; no refactoring needed.
